text:
Market today: Mixed macro. June payrolls came in soft at 57,000 - well below expectations - which is a double-edged sword for tech: rate-cut hopes tick up, but it also signals genuine growth slowdown. Your chip/tech names are sensitive to both. Tesla's strong delivery beat is a positive sentiment read for high-growth names broadly.

Your holdings: CRWD is the emergency - you're down 73.4% and the stop is listed at 590 on a $191 stock. That stop is completely disconnected from current price; you have no active protection. Fix this first. SOXL is down 14.9% and approaching its $200 stop - leveraged decay plus a soft macro print could accelerate the move. SPAL is down 13.3% with a stop only 2% away at $21.38 - on margin, that's a hair-trigger. Note margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: Whether the soft jobs number turns into a risk-off move into Thursday's open, and whether SMH/SOXL hold above their stops if chip sentiment wobbles.

Bottom line: Fix CRWD's broken stop and protect your leveraged positions before thinking about anything new.
plays: CRWD: Your stop at 590 is useless - price is at 191. Set a real stop immediately, just below a nearby support level you identify, or cut the position. This is your highest-priority action today.
SOXL: Stop is at 200, price at 216.50 - only 7.6% of cushion on a 3x leveraged ETF after a soft jobs print. Tighten or be ready to act fast if semis sell off.
No new buys today - your two most urgent jobs are fixing CRWD's stop and protecting SOXL/SPAL before the macro tone settles post-jobs report.
